description: One-pass review of crate secretx-azure-kv (/home/mark/PROJECT/crate-secretsx/secretx-azure-kv). Filed by /review-rusty-crate.
notes:
Methodology: read-only review of secretx-azure-kv against typespec-0.14 ErrorKind, azure_security_keyvault_secrets-0.14 Secret model, and sister-crate patterns (aws-sm, gcp-sm, hashicorp-vault). cargo clippy clean as of 2026-05-07T17:35:30-07:00.
Decision: did not file the 'serde_json::Value vs SecretValue::extract_field for ?field= extraction' concern as a per-crate finding because the same pattern is used in aws-sm and gcp-sm, and the crate docstring explicitly accepts this trade-off (the SDK already leaks the secret as a non-Zeroizing String). This is a workspace-level concern, not an azure-kv concern.
Decision: did not file 'inventory::submit! looks unused' because the same pattern appears in every backend crate; that is a workspace-level documentation concern, not specific to azure-kv.
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — vault_name validation gap (P0) is the only finding that could become a CVE if URI input is ever exposed to less-trusted callers; chained-credential silent-fallback (P1) is the most likely source of operator-visible 'wrong cred used' confusion; Connection ErrorKind miss (P0) will degrade reliability under network blips. None are fast-and-loose; all are surface bugs in tightly-scoped helpers.
Correctness confidence: 70% chance of missed bug. Least confident flags: behavior under Azure KV soft-delete (the SDK may distinguish 404 from 410-Gone differently, not investigated); behavior under HTTP 401/403 (currently classified as Backend permanent — likely correct but not directly tested). Uncertainty driven by: no live Azure environment for end-to-end retry-loop validation; azure_core 0.35 is 0.x and pre-1.0 semantics may shift.
Idiom rating: ACCEPTABLE — error-handling repetition (crate-secretsx-bdu0.22) is the only idiomatic-Rust finding with engineering basis worth fixing as part of normal maintenance. Trait impls, async patterns, and ownership are all correct. ChainedCredential's manual Vec/Debug is mildly non-idiomatic (crate-secretsx-bdu0.19, .20) but does not produce real issues. No unsafe, no macro abuse, no lifetime gymnastics.
Rust idiom confidence: 85% chance of missed issue. Least confident flags: 'as Arc<dyn>' coercion (crate-secretsx-bdu0.21) — would need to test the code without 'as' to confirm coercion still fires across the .map() boundary; it should, but I did not compile-test the variant. Uncertainty driven by: not running cargo expand on async_trait macros, which can hide subtleties in Send bounds and lifetimes.
Pass complete. Findings: 24. Mix:
title: Review: secretx-azure-kv rusty pass
